title: Research Brief: PM operating rhythm pain points
research_question: Where do PMs lose time and confidence in recurring operating work across planning, status, and follow-through?
summary: PMs lose time when recurring operating outputs depend on manual reconstruction across fragmented sources and stale updates.
strategic_implications: A trusted operating-state layer can differentiate ProductOS beyond document generation.
Workflow reliability is a strategic adoption requirement, not just an implementation detail.

insights.statement: Fragmented source systems increase PM effort because status, decisions, and blockers must be manually reconciled.
Automated outputs are useful only when the underlying state is current enough to trust.

contradictions.statement: Some PMs accept lower-confidence drafts for speed while others require high traceability before any reuse.

synthesis_provenance: Synthesized from consultant report notes, app review signals, and structured PM pain-point observations.
